reasoning: The highest credible estimate for US AMR deaths remains the CDC's 2019 report of 35,000 (or 48,000 if including C. diff). The Lancet's comprehensive September 2024 GRAM study on global AMR trends does not indicate a massive spike in US deaths. A jump to 70,000 would require a roughly 45–100% increase from the prior baselines. While the CDC may release a new AMR report in late 2025 (extrapolating from the 6-year gap between the 2013 and 2019 reports), prior CDC updates during the COVID-19 pandemic recorded only a ~15% increase in hospital-onset AMR infections. With only two months left until the resolution deadline and no evidence of deaths nearing the 70,000 threshold, this outcome is highly improbable. I am bounding my estimate at 0.05 per the rule that probabilities must be between 0.05 and 0.95.
batch_queries: Will the number of deaths from antibiotic-resistant infections per year double by December 31, 2025?
